pages.raw_text:
(Scripps News Association.)


DENVER, June 18.-The public
land convention was opened this
morning by Governor Buchtell.
Eight hundred delegates from
Washington, Nevada, North and
South Dakota, Idaho, New Mexico
Arizona, Wyoming, Oklahoma and
California were in attendance. Gar.
field, Pinchot, Ballinger and Newell,
representing the administration, ar.
rived last night.


Governor Buchtell, in his ads
dress, praised the policy of Roose-
welt He said:


I'VE meet to discuss the relation
of separate states to public land.
We must frame board program to
aim at the speedy development Of
unoccupied lands. The east still
thinks we have nothing but cow
boys and Indians out west. People
in the congested quarters should be
encouraged to occupy tracts of pub
lie lands"


Chief Forester Pinchot expressed
Roosevelt's conclusions on the TIM
Der question. He said there Is still
a great supply of timber in sight,
and Roosevelt believed the inter-
ests of the nation would be best
conserved IL large tracts we're left
in government control instead of in
the hands of rich men. He denied
that the president, in creating for.
est reserves, increased the prices to
the consumer. and argued that the
protection of irrigation in the west
justifies the forest policy.
